top_signals.title: US, Iran Renew Conflict With Strikes on Bridge, Desalination Plant
top_signals.summary: The United States struck bridges and an airport in Iran on Friday, provoking Tehran to respond by hitting a power and desalination plant in Kuwait, as the warring foes risked further escalation by expanding their targets to include infrastructure.At sea…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: LNG Shipments Through Strait of Hormuz Grind to a Halt as Conflict Escalates
top_signals.summary: Exports of liquefied natural gas via the Strait of Hormuz appear to have ground to a halt over the past three days, data from tanker-tracking firms have suggested, while oil tanker traffic has shrunk considerably, Reuters reported today. ING analysts, meanwhile, wrote in a note today that the latest hostilities would have a more serious impact on gas markets than oil markets, based on the previous rate of export recovery in the Gulf following the June ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran. “The post?MoU rebound in vessel traffic…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Cadeler Welcomes Wind Ace WTIV to its Fleet
top_signals.summary: Cadeler has taken delivery of Wind Ace, its 11th offshore wind turbine installation vessel (WTIV) and the second of three A-class newbuilds.Built over the past two years at the COSCO Shipping Offshore shipyard in Qidong, China, the vessel was delivered on schedule and within budget…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: What Are Mineral Rights? The Complete Guide to Owning, Leasing and Selling Them
top_signals.summary: Millions of Americans own oil and gas mineral rights and have no real idea what that means. Some inherited a folder of yellowed documents from a grandparent's estate. Some bought a piece of land and assumed everything under it came with the deed. Some get a small check in the mail every few months and couldn't explain why if asked. Mineral rights are one of the most valuable and least understood assets in the country, and a whole industry has built itself around that gap in knowledge. Landmen, mineral buyers and self-styled "finders" all make a…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: India Proposes Stricter Vehicle Emission Rules To Cut Oil Consumption
top_signals.summary: The Indian government has proposed stricter fuel efficiency regulations for passenger vehicles under the Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFE)-III norms, with the new fuel standards set to take effect on April 1, 2027. Released by the Ministry of Power for public consultation, these rules aim to lower vehicular emissions, decrease reliance on imported crude oil, and shrink the nation's rising oil import bill.  To capture real-world emissions accurately, the framework will transition from the Modified Indian Driving Cycle (MIDC) to the more…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Segro Rejects £13.5 Billion Cash and Share Prologis Offer
top_signals.summary: Prologis Inc. has increased its outline bid to buy Segro Plc to £13.5 billion ($18.2 billion) and offered a series of sweeteners as it seeks to tempt shareholders after the UK landlord’s board rejected the latest terms.

top_signals.title: FTSE 100 property giant Segro rejects third Prologis bid
top_signals.summary: Segro has rejected a second and third takeover bid from US rival Prologis, as the tussle between the two property giants steps up a gear. The FTSE 100 real estate firm rejected Prologis’ latest bid last week, which amounted to £9.93 per share and valued the group’s total shares at £13.5bn.  Prologis said its latest [...]
